description: Package Electron desktop app with electron-builder for Windows, bundle relay server inside app, auto-start relay on launch with port selection.
steps: Add electron-builder to apps/desktop as dev dependency and configure build section in package.json
Add bundle script to relay-server using esbuild to produce a single CJS file
Create relay server process manager in desktop main process
Modify apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts to auto-start and stop the relay server
Add build scripts (pack, dist, dist:win) to apps/desktop/package.json
Add root-level dist and pack scripts
Expose relay server status to renderer via IPC
acceptance_criteria: npm run build compiles all packages without errors
Relay server is bundled into apps/desktop/resources/relay-server.cjs
Main process has relay server process manager with port selection
verification: Run npm install and confirm exit code 0
Run npm run build and confirm exit code 0
Run npm test and confirm all tests pass
findings: All acceptance criteria met. npm install, build, and test all pass cleanly. esbuild bundles the relay server (149KB) into apps/desktop/resources/relay-server.cjs. electron-builder v25 installed successfully. The RelayServerManager uses child_process.fork() with port scanning (3000-3009). The main process auto-starts relay on app ready and kills it on window-all-closed. Cannot test actual electron-builder packaging (requires Windows for .exe output) but configuration is complete and validated.
